title: pi-workflows filter-doc & bundled-spec-validation claim evaluation — both TRUE
question: Are two dogfooding claims true: (1) the pi-workflows ${{ }} expression filters are drastically misdocumented; (2) most bundled workflow specs fail validation — and what is the root cause and class of each?
method: code-inspection (expression.ts / template.ts / workflow-sdk.ts / README / SKILL.md / skill-narrative.md) + empirical validateWorkflow run over all 15 bundled specs at repo root against the active .context substrate + registry read (read-config installed_blocks) + prior-art sweep of .context via the pi-context CLI (filter-block-items).
findings_summary: Both claims verdict TRUE against source and a real validator run. CLAIM 1 (filters misdocumented): expression.ts registers exactly 10 ${{ }} filters (duration, currency, json, length, keys, filter, last, first, slugify, shell); FILTER_NAMES = Object.keys(FILTERS) is re-exported as the SDK filterNames() and is the source the filter-names validation check consumes. Three doc surfaces (README.md:95-104, SKILL.md:286-288, skill-narrative.md:40-42) advertise a ~30-name list in the ${{ }} expression context; 20 of those names exist in neither the expression layer nor the stock-Nunjucks template layer (template.ts registers zero custom filters, so the phantom names are not mislabeled Nunjucks builtins — Nunjucks supplies only 8 of the 20). An unknown filter throws at runtime (expression.ts:110-114); authoring validation only warns. No bundled spec uses a phantom filter, so the trap is latent — it bites an author who writes a new spec trusting the docs. Class: documentation-ahead-of-implementation; the prose lists bypass the registry-derived single source of truth. CLAIM 2 (bundled specs fail validation): a real validateWorkflow run over all 15 specs reproduces 8 invalid / 7 warnings-only, matching the claim's per-spec breakdown exactly (self-implement and typed-analysis, which the claim omits, are also warnings-only). Causal refinement: the missing block schemas (project, architecture, handoff, inventory — confirmed absent from installed_blocks) are deliberately downgraded to WARNINGS (workflow-sdk.ts:483-492) and do NOT drive invalid status. The invalid verdicts are error-level input-contract / template-alignment checks: inputSchema-required (:597-614), template-alignment (:651-652), and the sharpest instance, do-gap step implement referencing spec.intent/spec.tasks/spec.context_needed — fields absent from the current schema (name, description, files, depends_on, acceptance_criteria, parallel_safe, estimated_complexity). Three distinct classes are present: (a) catalog/substrate-vocabulary drift (warning-level; sibling of FGAP-065 on the pi-context side); (b) genuinely stale schema shape (error, do-gap); (c) input-contract drift (error, dominant error source). For block schemas, validation is more lenient than runtime (warning vs a hard write failure only when a step actually writes an uninstalled block: target); for input-contract/template errors, validation catches at authoring time what runtime would hit at dispatch/render. Prior-art sweep: no .context item tracks either claim; nearest non-duplicates are FGAP-065, TASK-103/FGAP-127, issue-011, FGAP-104. Filed from this evaluation: FGAP-131 (Claim 1) and FGAP-132 (Claim 2), the latter related to FGAP-065.

citations.label: stock Nunjucks env, zero custom filters
SKILL source carrying the same list
block: schema ref -> warning; inputSchema-required error; template-alignment error
full evaluation: per-spec table, phantom-name derivation, class characterizations, prior-art
